commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of the disruptive source lifecycle and copied-disk credential boundary; no narrow automated code repair was identified.; Cleared: No concrete supply-chain issue or line-level security bug was found; credential rehydration remains a maintainer-visible merge risk because the feature copies a Windows disk before rotating access material. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds direct Azure Windows managed OS-disk checkpoint creation, snapshot-backed fork provisioning, credential/VNC rehydration, Azure scope propagation, docs, and Go tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a feature PR. The PR body gives exact-head live Azure validation for checkpoint creation, source restart, fork readiness, credential rotation, tunnel-only VNC, WSL2 health, and cleanup.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of the disruptive source lifecycle and copied-disk credential boundary; no narrow automated code repair was identified.

target.title: feat(azure): fork windows leases from snapshots
target.body:
## Summary

- add direct Azure Windows OS-disk checkpoints and snapshot-backed forks
- require explicit `--no-reboot=false`, deallocate for a consistent snapshot, then restart the source VM
- rotate SSH host keys, SSH login, Windows login, and TightVNC service credentials on each fork
- preserve loopback-only VNC and carry the snapshot subscription, resource group, region, and OS type into the fork
- document the disruptive checkpoint contract and managed-disk requirement

## Why

Prepared Azure Windows desktop leases are expensive to bootstrap repeatedly. A native OS-disk snapshot gives later leases a fast starting point, but only if the fork is treated as a new machine: identity and access credentials must rotate, VNC must remain tunnel-only, and Azure resource scope must follow the snapshot.

## Verification

- `go test ./internal/providers/azure ./internal/cli`
- `go vet ./...`
- focused race tests pass; the full repository race run only reports the pre-existing macOS temporary-directory cleanup race
- GitHub Actions: Go, Apple VZ, Worker, Scripts, Docs, and Release Check all pass on `72d5e2bfd06585b495febddbab306548473d406d`

## Live Azure Proof

Validated the exact PR head `72d5e2bfd06585b495febddbab306548473d406d` against real Azure resources:

- Acquired source lease `cbx_c76dc7a1d1ba` as a `Standard_D4s_v5` Azure Windows desktop and completed an end-to-end Sqatch bootstrap run (`ba4fcd25-83cb-469e-85a0-899fcd17e3ad`, pass).
- Created OS-disk checkpoint `chk_e604e3ad04f2a596`; checkpoint creation deallocated the source for consistency and returned it to a reachable running state.
- Forked canary lease `cbx_b9fa4aef33bf` from that checkpoint and reached ready Windows execution.
- Verified fork identity rotation: SSH host/login identity, Windows login password, and TightVNC service password differed from the source credentials. Secret values were not recorded in the PR.
- Verified VNC remained tunnel-only: direct public TCP 5900 timed out, while the SSH-forwarded loopback endpoint authenticated and produced a clean desktop screenshot.
- Verified WSL2 and the Sqatch/ASQA target doctor were healthy on the canary.
- Explicitly stopped the canary after proof collection; no retained validation lease was left active.

This covers the requested source restart, fork readiness, credential-boundary, and tunnel-only VNC behavior on the exact reviewed head.

## Stack

1. #698 Azure Windows snapshot forks (this PR)
2. [#731](https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/pull/731) Azure snapshot lifecycle performance
target.author: fcoury-oai

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
